Dear ks55, The Law of Here and Now: We cannot be present if we are looking backward to examine what was or forward to worry about the future.  We need to free ourselves from the resentment past or worrisome future in order to capture the opportunity of present. No point keeps dwelling in the sorrowful past or fearful future; we need to live in the present. OTB, DK66 and others in this forum did not promote JAKS with “Sailang” or “Margin finance” likes it was promoted before.  Everyone in this forum is welcome to digest whatever information in this forum and make their own intelligent and informed decision. If you are willing lets take a further look into 2022 ! Jaks is given the option to acquire additional 10% of HaiDuong Power Station, making it a very significant 40% partner. The dateline is 3years from COD at a cost of about rm250M. (This is another juicy part of the contract not frequently talk about yet ) Given that projected cashflow for 2021 itself is sufficient for the acquisition..it is no brainer Jaks will make the acquisition. And I think they will do it sooner than later..probably by 2022. BTW, Jaks will have 3 board seats rep and CPECC will have 2 board seat for the first 3years. So what will be the impact ?  Net profit for 2021 (Full production and 30% stake) = RM 287 million. EPS = 287/643 = 0.446 Base on PER = 10, the target price of Jaks should be RM 4.46 in 2021.  Net profit for 2022 (Full production and 40% stake) = RM 382 million. EPS = 382/643 = 59.5 Base on PER = 10, the target price of Jaks should be RM5.95 in 2022.  When the HaiDuong plant is fully operational by 2nd half 2020 and Jaks acquired 40% stake, I suspect the PE will go well above 10 ! Anyone is free to debates the numbers or facts ,better still provides your rational or data but pls do so with a good spirit.

Jaks is still an "up trending" stock, NO REASON TO CUT LOSS. Base on TA, we cut loss if the stock is "down trending" stock. Please read your chart clearly, the share price of Jaks is still very far higher than 70-day SMA.  "Down trending stock" - The left hand side is higher than right hand side. "Up trending stock" - The right hand side is higher than left hand side. Jaks is still an "up trending" stock.  Start of "up trending" - The share price breaks 3-month high (I used 70-days SMA for 3 month period). The share price must be higher than 70-day SMA. Presently, the share price of Jaks is higher than 70-day SMA. Hence Jaks is an "up trending" stock.  I worked as a Technical chart trainer for 10 years in Malaysia, China and Hong Kong. I conducted training course for students in these 3 countries.  Thank you.
